title: Inheritance and Polymorphism
learningObjectives: Explain Inheritance and Polymorphism with clear interview-ready wording
Implement Inheritance and Polymorphism using compilable Java examples for Day 10
Identify and prevent common bugs related to Inheritance and Polymorphism
Discuss design and performance trade-offs for Inheritance and Polymorphism
Solve at least one realistic exercise based on Inheritance and Polymorphism
Answer conceptual, coding, and scenario interview questions confidently

sections.title: Why Inheritance and Polymorphism matters
sections.content:
Inheritance and Polymorphism is a high-impact topic for Day 10. It directly supports OOP principles and core Java APIs and appears in interviews as a depth-check area.

Why this matters in practice:
- Teams rely on this concept for clean implementation decisions.
- Mistakes here create maintainability and production-risk issues.
- Strong understanding improves both coding rounds and system discussions.

Learning strategy for this day:
1. Define the concept clearly in one line.
2. Implement one minimal Java example.
3. Validate at least one edge/failure case.
4. Explain one trade-off (simplicity vs flexibility, speed vs safety, etc.).
5. Rephrase your answer as if explaining to an interviewer.

If you can explain Inheritance and Polymorphism clearly and implement it without copy-paste, you are interview-ready for this unit.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Inheritance and Polymorphism
sections.content:
### Day 10 Topic: Inheritance and Polymorphism
This lesson belongs to Phase 2 (OOP principles and core Java APIs) and targets Beginner level expectations.

### Core Concept
Start by defining Inheritance and Polymorphism in precise terms, then connect it to runtime behavior and design impact.

### Internal Understanding
For this topic, interviewers expect more than API recall. They look for:
- correct mental model
- edge-case awareness
- clean implementation choices
- trade-off reasoning

### Practical Implementation Approach
1. Build a baseline solution that is correct and readable.
2. Add validation for boundary, null/empty, or failure scenarios.
3. Measure or reason about time/space or operational cost.
4. Refactor into maintainable units.

### Interview Structure (recommended)
- What is it?
- Why is it needed?
- One practical Java example
- One common pitfall
- One better alternative (if constraints change)

### Production Perspective
In real systems, correctness alone is not enough. Inheritance and Polymorphism should also support observability, safe changes, and team readability.

### Revision Checklist
- Can you explain this without notes?
- Can you code it from memory?
- Can you test one edge case quickly?
- Can you justify your approach in 30-60 seconds?

### Satyverse — deeper path (theory → practice)
**Inheritance models shared behaviour; polymorphism lets callers depend on supertypes.** Prefer abstract or interface types in method signatures so you can swap implementations (testing, feature flags).

**Override rules:** `@Override` catches signature typos; covariant return types are allowed; widening access is allowed, narrowing is not.

**super vs this:** `super.method()` to extend parent behaviour in `@Override` methods — template method pattern starts here.

**Avoid deep hierarchies:** more than 3–4 levels usually signals missing composition — extract policies or strategies instead.

**Interview:** explain dynamic dispatch: JVM selects the implementation at runtime based on the *actual* object type, not the reference type.

sections.title: Basic Walkthrough
sections.description: Subclass extends superclass: inherits fields and can override methods.

sections.title: Intermediate Implementation
sections.description: Runtime polymorphism: method selection uses the object’s actual class (Dog), not the variable type.

sections.title: Advanced Production Pattern
sections.description: Abstract method forces subclasses to implement; base class defines shared algorithm skeleton.

sections.title: Architecture View - Day 10
sections.description: Visual model for Inheritance and Polymorphism

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
items: Treating Inheritance and Polymorphism as memorization instead of implementation practice.
Skipping edge-case tests and validating only happy path.
Using unclear naming and tightly coupled method logic.
Giving interview answers without concrete examples.

sections.title: Exercise - Practice Inheritance and Polymorphism
sections.problem:
Implement a Java solution for **Inheritance and Polymorphism** with practical constraints.

Requirements:
1. Handle at least 5 test inputs.
2. Include one explicit edge/failure case.
3. Print deterministic output for verification.
4. Add short comments for decision points.
5. Keep the solution interview-readable and maintainable.
hints: Start with correctness first, optimize later.
Add one guard clause for invalid input.
Refactor method names before final answer.
sections.solution:
// Day 10 Inheritance and Polymorphism - reference solution intentionally omitted in prompt.
// Reuse the "Advanced Production Pattern" code block above as base and add edge-case handling.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is inheritance in Java?
conceptual.answer: Subclass extends superclass, inheriting fields and methods (subject to access rules).

conceptual.question: What is polymorphism?
conceptual.answer: A superclass reference can point to subclass objects; overridden methods are invoked dynamically.

conceptual.question: What is dynamic method dispatch?
conceptual.answer: JVM selects implementation based on actual object type at runtime for instance methods.

conceptual.question: Can constructors be inherited?
conceptual.answer: No—subclasses call super(...) but do not inherit superclass constructors as their own.

conceptual.question: What is @Override for?
conceptual.answer: Compiler checks that you truly override an instance method—catches signature mistakes.

conceptual.question: What is hiding of static methods?
conceptual.answer: Subclass defines static with same name—compile-time binding, not polymorphic.

conceptual.question: What is covariant return in override?
conceptual.answer: Subclass may return narrower type than superclass method.

conceptual.question: Why avoid deep inheritance trees?
conceptual.answer: Hard to reason, fragile base class; composition often clearer.

conceptual.question: What is abstract class vs concrete?
conceptual.answer: Abstract cannot be instantiated; may contain abstract and concrete methods.

conceptual.question: What is Object.equals contract?
conceptual.answer: Reflexive, symmetric, transitive, consistent; with hashCode consistency.

conceptual.question: What is Liskov Substitution Principle?
conceptual.answer: Subtypes must be substitutable for base types without breaking expectations.

conceptual.question: What happens if subclass constructor omits super()?
conceptual.answer: Compiler inserts implicit super() if no args—fails if no matching superclass constructor.

conceptual.question: Can you make private method “override”?
conceptual.answer: No—private methods are not inherited; subclass method is unrelated.

conceptual.question: What is final class impact on inheritance?
conceptual.answer: Cannot subclass—useful for security and immutability guarantees.

conceptual.question: What is instanceof with sealed hierarchy?
conceptual.answer: Exhaustive switches possible with compiler help.

codeBased.question: Abstract Animal with abstract speak; Dog implements.
codeBased.answer: abstract class Animal { abstract void speak(); }

codeBased.question: Demonstrate wrong overload vs override.
codeBased.answer: Same name different params is overload, not override.

codeBased.question: Polymorphic collection List<Animal> with Dog instances.
Use template method pattern with final execute.

seniorScenario.question: Hibernate proxy breaks equals with inheritance—issue?
seniorScenario.answer: Class identity vs instance; use business keys or composition.

seniorScenario.question: JSON polymorphic deserialization—risk?
seniorScenario.answer: Open type gadgets; use allowlists, @JsonTypeInfo carefully.

seniorScenario.question: Breaking API: making method final in base—impact?
seniorScenario.answer: Subclasses can no longer override—major version bump.

seniorScenario.question: Inheritance for code reuse only—smell?
seniorScenario.answer: Often composition + delegation clearer than “is-a” lie.

seniorScenario.question: Testing abstract class behavior?
seniorScenario.answer: Concrete test subclass or Mockito spy partial mocks.

seniorScenario.question: Diamond problem in Java?
seniorScenario.answer: Single inheritance for classes; interfaces with default methods need explicit resolution rules.

wrongAnswers: static methods participate in dynamic dispatch like instance methods.
You can override private methods in subclasses.
Constructors are inherited from the parent class.
Polymorphism means the variable type decides which method runs.
final class can still be extended by nested subclasses arbitrarily.
equals can be symmetric even if subclasses add fields without changing contract.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Area | Rule | Interview Tip | Gotcha |
|---|---|---|---|
| Concept | Define Inheritance and Polymorphism in 1-2 lines | Start with purpose first | Vague definitions |
| Code | Write minimal compilable example | Explain expected output | Hidden edge-case bug |
| Validation | Test boundary + invalid input | Mention test strategy | Only happy-path tests |
| Trade-off | Compare 2 approaches | Link choice to constraints | "Depends" with no criteria |
| Phase Context | Align with phase 2 focus | Show progression mindset | Isolated-topic thinking |
